child_policy: No child ideas spawned because decision is rejected.
compact_artifact_note: Only compact summary statistics are retained; no per-day records, database files, caches, or helper scripts are retained in the repository.

data_source.provenance: Configured paper-data access was used with credential values redacted; evaluation used real market data, no CSV, no daemon, and no orders.

evaluation_protocol.costs: 10 bps one-way turnover primary; 5/20 bps sensitivity; roundtrip applied as two one-way turns.
evaluation_protocol.entry: last trading day of December close

falsifier: Reject if fewer than 8 usable events, weak/negative distribution after 10 bps one-way costs, dependence on one year, failure versus January/ToM/SPY/reversal/TSMOM baselines, or redundant high correlation to library.
hypothesis: Late-December tax-loss selling pressure in large YTD losers rebounds over the first five January trading days; evaluated separately for ETFs and large-cap equities.

orthogonality.reason: Existing watchlist artifacts in this worktree expose compact aggregate metrics but no aligned annual event-return streams for this AR-123 Dec/Jan event horizon; orthogonality not used to rescue rejected candidate.

candidate_pool_descriptions.equity: Static large/mega-cap equity candidate pool selected as liquid, widely traded US listings with long Alpaca coverage; no current-index membership claim is used.
candidate_pool_descriptions.etf: Broad ex-ante ETF pool across index, sector, industry, bond, credit, commodity, international, factor/style, and real-asset exposures; final selected set is coverage/liquidity-filtered before loser ranking.

filters.selection_metric: median dollar volume over available Alpaca daily bars, after coverage screens; no return-performance filter.

limitations: Static candidate pools do not include delisted securities, so survivorship bias is reduced by coverage/liquidity discipline but not eliminated.
Selection used historical coverage/liquidity only, not current index membership claims or return performance.

warnings: Research/backtesting only; no daemon and no orders.
Alpaca daily bars are used as provided by qfa gateway; adjustment status is not explicitly controlled in this repo version.
Annual event sample is sparse even with 10 usable events; distributional metrics are low power.
